verdict_reasoning: HSP60 (HSPD1) is canonically a mitochondrial matrix chaperonin. However, it has well-documented cell-state-induced surface translocation: on tumor cells and stressed/apoptotic cells, HSP60 is displayed on the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane. Multiple studies have identified surface HSP60 as a target on cancer cells (targeted by antibodies, ADC-like approaches), and it is externalized during immunogenic cell death and stress responses. This ecto-HSP60 pool is the therapeutic surface story, qualifying it as contextual/cell_state_induced.
